subprocess_flow.description: The Project Manager or Delivery Lead logs into the MoJ central AI and Data registration portal using their corporate credentials. This portal serves as the single entry point for all data-driven initiatives to ensure centralized visibility and tracking from the earliest stage of the project lifecycle.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a standardized and secure entry point for logging AI project intent.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Unauthorized access to the registration system.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Single Sign-On (SSO) and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

subprocess_flow.description: The user inputs core project details including the project name, department, primary objectives, and a high-level description of the intended AI or data usage. This metadata is used by the Data Office to categorize the project and determine the appropriate governance path based on complexity and potential impact.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To capture the essential scope and intent of the AI project for governance triage.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Incomplete or vague project descriptions hindering triage.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory field validation and descriptive tooltips in the portal.

governance_requirements: Data quality standards for registration entries

subprocess_flow.description: The Project Manager uploads documentation or specifies the datasets required for the project, including source systems and preliminary data sensitivity levels. This allows the Data Office to perform an early assessment of data protection requirements and potential security constraints.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide the Data Office with early insight into the data assets involved.
subprocess_flow.scope: Preliminary data sourcing and sensitivity identification.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Sensitive data included in the registration brief itself.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Automated scanning for PII/Sensitive terms in attachments.

governance_requirements: Alignment with MoJ Data Handling Policy

subprocess_flow.description: The Data Office Governance Team reviews the registration submission for completeness and validity. They check for potential overlaps with existing MoJ projects and identify if the project involves high-risk AI use cases that require immediate escalation to the Ethics Committee or DPO.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To ensure the quality of registration data and identify high-risk projects early.

step_risks_and_controls.control: SLA-based tracking and automated reminders for the governance team.

governance_requirements: Central AI model inventory entry criteria

subprocess_flow.description: Upon triage approval, the project is formally recorded in the MoJ Central AI Model Inventory (e.g., Collibra). A unique Project ID is generated, which must be used in all subsequent governance, security, and data quality documentation throughout the project lifecycle.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To maintain a single source of truth for all AI projects within the MoJ.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Duplicate project entries in the inventory.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Unique ID constraints and automated duplicate detection logic.

subprocess_flow.description: The Data Office issues a formal confirmation to the Project Manager, providing the unique Project ID and a summary of the next mandatory governance steps (e.g., Data Classification, DPIA). This step ensures the project team is aware of their compliance obligations.
success_criteria: Confirmation email sent to Project Manager
subprocess_flow.purpose: To formally close the registration step and provide compliance guidance.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Project team unaware of next governance steps.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Automated issuance of a 'Governance Roadmap' with the confirmation.
